agl-header.note: header facet of agl-card and agl-multiselect; the 'facet card' and 'multiselect' scenarios assert it projects into the slot

agl-footer.note: footer facet of agl-card; asserted by the 'facet card' scenario

agl-motion.note: the transition wrapper of every overlay; the 'facet dialog' scenario opens a dialog, so it is constructed and torn down

[aglMenuItemContent].note: menu constructs one per item; the 'menu' scenario clicks an item and therefore goes through it

agl-tieredMenuSub.note: each submenu level is one instance; the 'tieredmenu' scenario opens a branch and clicks a nested item

agl-selectItem.note: one per option in the overlay; the 'select' scenario opens the overlay and picks one

li[aglMultiSelectItem].note: one per option in the overlay; the 'multiselect' scenario selects two items

agl-cellEditor.note: in the #sec-table-adv block; the 'table advanced' scenario clicks a cell to switch it from display to input mode

agl-columnFilter.note: in the #sec-table-adv block; the 'table advanced' scenario types then presses Enter, taking the row count from 3 to 1

agl-columnFilterFormElement.note: the real filter input when display=row; it is the field the 'table advanced' scenario types into

[aglTreeTableBody].note: treetable constructs it to render the table body; every visible row goes through it

agl-treeTableToggler.note: the expand/collapse control; the 'treetable' scenario clicks it, taking 1 row to 3

agl-treeTableCellEditor.note: in the #sec-treetable-scroll block; the 'treetable scroll + cell edit' scenario clicks a cell and edits the value

[ttScrollableView].note: the #sec-treetable-scroll block enables scrollable; the scenario asserts .p-treetable-scrollable-view exists
